sourceMovie.title: Enchanted
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Enchanted works as a comedy movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The comedy and family blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Shrek 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Enchanted clicked for you, Shrek 2 (2004) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and family, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Charming
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Enchanted clicked for you, Charming (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and family, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Beauty and the Beast
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Enchanted clicked for you, Beauty and the Beast (2017) is a strong next stop: both trade in family and fantasy, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Enchanted?
faq.answer: Try Shrek 2, Charming, Beauty and the Beast—each shares DNA with Enchanted while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Enchanted?
faq.answer: Enchanted (2007) is best described as Comedy, Family, Fantasy, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Enchanted part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Enchanted Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
